skill.name: evaluation
skill.description: Build evaluation frameworks for agent systems. Use when testing agent performance, validating context engineering choices, or measuring improvements over time.
skill.summary: Build structured evaluation frameworks for agent quality, regressions, and context engineering changes.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, ordinary evaluation terms, and Python dictionary key access. No evidence found of shell execution, weak cryptography, credential material, network reconnaissance, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences and Python examples. They do not execute commands and show no user input passed to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced Markdown examples or prose boundaries, not executable Ruby backticks. No subprocess, shell, or command invocation is present in the cited locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Overmatches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm findings match ordinary words such as description, dimensions, and methods in evaluation guidance. No cryptographic API or weak algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain evaluation prose and rubric data, not cryptographic routines. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Certificate or Key Material Not Present
low_findings.description: The certificate and key findings are ordinary Python dictionary key access in evaluation summaries. No private key, certificate, token, or secret material appears at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations use rubric.keys() to iterate dictionary keys. This is unrelated to credential files or certificate material.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Used in Benign Context
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are caused by normal evaluation text and skill names. No system probing, network scanning, host enumeration, or external network access is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss agent evaluation concepts and cross-linked skill names. I did not find reconnaissance commands or network scanning behavior.

content.value_statement: Agent behavior is hard to measure because valid paths can differ between runs. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build rubrics, test sets, and monitoring plans.
actual_capabilities: Defines multi-dimensional rubrics for factual accuracy, completeness, citations, source quality, and tool efficiency.
Guides test set design across simple, medium, complex, and extended agent tasks.
Explains how to compare agent configurations and context engineering strategies.
Provides example Python structures for scoring, test management, evaluation runs, and monitoring.
Supports regression tracking through pass rates, dimension averages, and failure summaries.
limitations: Example evaluator logic uses simple heuristics and is not a complete production judge.
The skill does not provide integrations with external observability platforms.
Evaluation quality depends on clear ground truth, task design, and representative samples.
Human review is still needed for subtle failures, bias, and unusual edge cases.

use_cases.title: Create an agent quality gate
use_cases.description: Define rubric dimensions and pass thresholds before releasing a new agent workflow.

use_cases.title: Compare context engineering changes
use_cases.description: Run the same test set against different context strategies and compare quality and tool efficiency.

use_cases.title: Monitor production agent quality
use_cases.description: Sample real interactions, score outputs, and track pass rate trends over time.

prompt_templates.title: Build a basic rubric
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new agent evaluation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple evaluation rubric for my agent. Include factual accuracy, completeness, citation accuracy, source quality, and tool efficiency.

prompt_templates.title: Design a test set
prompt_templates.scenario: Create representative cases before evaluation runs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a test set for this agent workflow. Include simple, medium, complex, and very complex cases with expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare agent versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Measure whether a change improved quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two agent configurations using an outcome-focused evaluation plan. Include metrics, thresholds, and regression checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan continuous monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a continuous evaluation pipeline for a production agent. Include sampling, LLM-as-judge review, human review, alerts, and dashboard metrics.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a research agent that answers financial comparison questions.
output_examples.output: A weighted rubric with accuracy, completeness, citation quality, source quality, and tool efficiency scores. It also includes pass thresholds and review notes.

output_examples.input: Build tests for a customer support agent before release.
output: A stratified test set with simple account questions, medium troubleshooting cases, and complex multi-step escalation scenarios.
Expected outcomes are written as user-facing success criteria instead of exact response text.

output_examples.output: A monitoring plan that samples interactions, scores outputs, tracks pass rate changes, and sends alerts when quality drops.

best_practices: Evaluate outcomes and process quality instead of requiring one exact execution path.
Use representative samples from real user tasks and add known edge cases.
Combine automated judging with human review for high-risk or ambiguous outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on one overall score without dimension-level diagnostics.
Do not evaluate only short examples when the agent handles long context tasks.
Do not treat LLM-as-judge results as final for safety, compliance, or bias decisions.

faq.question: What does this skill help evaluate?
faq.answer: It helps evaluate agent outputs, tool usage, context strategies, regressions, and production quality trends.

faq.question: Does it run a complete evaluation platform?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and example structures, but production systems need real integrations and review workflows.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill content is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: How should I choose pass thresholds?
faq.answer: Start with risk and user impact. Higher-risk workflows need stricter thresholds and more human review.

faq.question: Why does it prefer outcome-focused evaluation?
faq.answer: Agents can take different valid paths. Outcome-focused scoring avoids failing useful answers for harmless process differences.

faq.question: Should human reviewers still be involved?
faq.answer: Yes. Human review catches subtle errors, bias, unusual edge cases, and failures that automated judging may miss.
